Name: Exeggcute
Effects: Search your deck for Exeggcute and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Six of them together form a full-fledged Pokémon. It’s often hunted by Crabrawler, but uses psychokinesis to drive it off.

Name: Alolan Exeggutor
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each type of basic Energy card in your discard pile. You can’t add more than 100 damage in this way.
Flavor-Text: As it grew taller and taller, it outgrew its reliance on psychic powers, while within it awakened the power of the sleeping dragon.

Name: Alolan Exeggutor
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each type of basic Energy card in your discard pile. You can’t add more than 100 damage in this way.
Flavor-Text: As it grew taller and taller, it outgrew its reliance on psychic powers, while within it awakened the power of the sleeping dragon.

Name: Snover
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a [F] Pokémon, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: In the spring, it grows berries with the texture of frozen treats around its belly.

Name: Abomasnow
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may attach a [G] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It lives a quiet life on mountains that are perpetually covered in snow. It hides itself by whipping up blizzards.

Name: Scatterbug
Ability-Effect: You can use this Ability only if you go second. Once during your first turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Spewpa and a Vivillon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: The powder that covers its body regulates its temperature, so it can live in any region or climate.

Name: Scatterbug
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for Vivillon and put it onto this Scatterbug to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: The powder that covers its body regulates its temperature, so it can live in any region or climate.

Name: Spewpa
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The beaks of birds can’t begin to scratch its stalwart body. To defend itself, it spews powder.

Name: Vivillon
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The patterns on this Pokémon’s wings depend on the climate and topography of its habitat. It scatters colorful scales.

Name: Skiddo
Effects: Attach a [G] Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If it has sunshine and water, it doesn’t need to eat, because it can generate energy from the leaves on its back.

Name: Gogoat
Effects: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.
This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: They inhabit mountainous regions. The leader of the herd is decided by a battle of clashing horns.

Name: Pheromosa
Effects: If you have only 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: One of the Ultra Beasts. It refuses to touch anything, perhaps because it senses some uncleanness in this world.

Name: Pheromosa
Effects: If you have only 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: One of the Ultra Beasts. It refuses to touch anything, perhaps because it senses some uncleanness in this world.

Name: Alolan Marowak
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has Alolan in its name.
Flavor-Text: Its custom is to mourn its lost companions. Mounds of dirt by the side of the road mark the graves of the Marowak.

Name: Heatran
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Boiling blood, like magma, circulates through its body. It makes its dwelling place in volcanic caves.

Name: Fennekin
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: As it walks, it munches on a twig in place of a snack. It intimidates opponents by puffing hot air out of its ears.

Name: Fennekin
Flavor-Text: As it walks, it munches on a twig in place of a snack. It intimidates opponents by puffing hot air out of its ears.

Name: Braixen
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When the twig is plucked from its tail, friction sets the twig alight. The flame is used to send signals to its allies.

Name: Delphox
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may leave your opponent’s Active Pokémon Burned.
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Using psychic power, it generates a fiery vortex of 5,400 degree Fahrenheit, incinerating foes swept into this whirl of flame.

Name: Litleo
Flavor-Text: They set off on their own from their pride and live by themselves to become stronger. These hot-blooded Pokémon are quick to fight.

Name: Pyroar
Ability-Effect: Whenever your opponent plays an Item or Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to this Pokémon.
Effects: If Lysandre Labs is in play, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: With fiery breath of more than 10,000 degrees Fahrenheit, they viciously threaten any challenger. The females protect the pride’s cubs.

Name: Palkia-GX
Effects: Move any number of Energy from your Benched Pokémon to this Pokémon.
This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Shuffle all Energy from each of your opponent’s Pokémon into their deck. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Froakie
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [W] Energy attached to it, it has no Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: It protects its skin by covering its body in delicate bubbles. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings.

Name: Froakie
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [W] Energy attached to it, it has no Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: It protects its skin by covering its body in delicate bubbles. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings.

Name: Froakie
Flavor-Text: It protects its skin by covering its body in delicate bubbles. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings.

Name: Frogadier
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its swiftness is unparalleled. It can scale a tower of more than 2,000 feet in a minute’s time.

Name: Frogadier
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its swiftness is unparalleled. It can scale a tower of more than 2,000 feet in a minute’s time.

Name: Greninja-GX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.
This attack does 130 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Clauncher
Flavor-Text: Through controlled expulsions of internal gas, it can expel water like a pistol shot. At close distances, it can shatter rock.

Name: Clawitzer
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Sharpshooting attack does 120 damage instead of 40.
This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: By expelling water from the nozzle in the back of its claw, it can move at a speed of 60 knots.

Name: Amaura
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: This calm Pokémon lived in a cold land where there were no violent predators like Tyrantrum.

Name: Aurorus
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Evolution Pokémon.
This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: Using its diamond-shaped crystals, it can instantly create a wall of ice to block an opponent’s attack.

Name: Bergmite
Effects: If your opponent has a Stadium card in play, discard it. If you do, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Using air of -150 degrees Fahrenheit, they freeze opponents solid. They live in herds above the snow line on mountains.

Name: Avalugg
Effects: Your opponent can’t play any Stadium cards from their hand during their next turn.
Flavor-Text: The way several Bergmite huddle on its back makes it look like an aircraft carrier made of ice.

Name: Volcanion Prism Star
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a [W] Energy card from your hand. If you do, your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Prism-Star-Rule: You can’t have more than 1 [*] card with the same name in your deck. If a [*] card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.
Flavor-Text: It expels its internal steam from the arms on its back. It has enough power to blow away a mountain.

Name: Dewpider
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: When it comes across enemies or potential prey, this Pokémon smashes its water-bubble-covered head into them.

Name: Araquanid
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
If 1 of your Pokémon used Bubble during your last turn, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Despite what its appearance suggests, it cares for others. If it finds vulnerable, weak Pokémon, it protectively brings them into its water bubble.

Name: Magnemite
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 [L] Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It sends out electromagnetic waves, which let it float through the air. Touching it while it’s eating electricity will give you a full-body shock.

Name: Magneton
Effects: This Pokémon can’t use Zap Cannon during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: It has about three times the electrical power of Magnemite. For some reason, outbreaks of this Pokémon happen when lots of sunspots appear.

Name: Magnezone
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [L] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Effects: This Pokémon can’t use Zap Cannon during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: As it zooms through the sky, this Pokémon seems to be receiving signals of unknown origin, while transmitting signals of unknown purpose.

Name: Helioptile
Flavor-Text: The frills on either side of its head have cells that generate electricity when exposed to sunlight.

Name: Heliolisk
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It stimulates its muscles with electricity, boosting the strength in its legs and enabling it to run 100 yards in five seconds.

Name: Xurkitree
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
If you have exactly 3 Prize cards remaining, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It appeared from the Ultra Wormhole. It raided a power plant, so people think it energizes itself with electricity.

Name: Xurkitree
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
If you have exactly 3 Prize cards remaining, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It appeared from the Ultra Wormhole. It raided a power plant, so people think it energizes itself with electricity.

Name: Rotom
Ability-Effect: If you have 9 or more Pokémon Tool cards in your discard pile, ignore all Energy in the attack cost of each of this Pokémon’s attacks.
Effects: This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

Name: Uxie
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks. That Pokémon can’t use that attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It is said that its emergence gave humans the intelligence to improve their quality of life.

Name: Mesprit
Ability-Effect: If you have Azelf in play, your opponent’s Pokémon in play have no Resistance.
Effects: If Uxie is on your Bench, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It sleeps at the bottom of a lake. Its spirit is said to leave its body to fly on the lake’s surface.

Name: Azelf
Effects: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only on your first turn. Shuffle 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon and all cards attached to it into their deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It is thought that Uxie, Mesprit and Azelf all came from the same egg.

Name: Espurr
Effects: Move an Energy from 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to another of their Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has enough psychic energy to blast everything within 300 feet of itself, but it has no control over its power.

Name: Meowstic
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This attack does 30 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The eyeball patterns on the interior of its ears emit psychic energy. It keeps the patterns tightly covered because that power is too immense.

Name: Honedge
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, put 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If anyone dares to grab its hilt, it wraps a blue cloth around that person’s arm and drains that person’s life energy completely.

Name: Honedge
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: If anyone dares to grab its hilt, it wraps a blue cloth around that person’s arm and drains that person’s life energy completely.

Name: Doublade
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on each of your opponent’s Pokémon that has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it.
Flavor-Text: The complex attack patterns of its two swords are unstoppable, even for an opponent greatly accomplished at swordplay.

Name: Aegislash
Effects: During your next turn, if the Defending Pokémon is damaged by an attack, it will be Knocked Out.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Apparently, it can detect the innate qualities of leadership. According to legend, whoever it recognizes is destined to become king.

Name: Inkay
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It flashes the light-emitting spots on its body, which drains its opponent’s will to fight. It takes the opportunity to scuttle away and hide.

Name: Inkay
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It flashes the light-emitting spots on its body, which drains its opponent’s will to fight. It takes the opportunity to scuttle away and hide.

Name: Malamar
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [P] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lures prey close with hypnotic motions, then wraps its tentacles around it before finishing it off with digestive fluids.

Name: Malamar
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [P] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lures prey close with hypnotic motions, then wraps its tentacles around it before finishing it off with digestive fluids.

Name: Skrelp
Flavor-Text: It looks just like rotten kelp. It hides from foes while storing up power for its evolution.

Name: Dragalge
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon. If both of them are tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Tales are told of ships that wander into seas where Dragalge live, never to return.

Name: Hoopa
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Item cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It gathers things it likes and passes them through its loop to teleport them to a secret place.

Name: Poipole
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is Knocked Out, your opponent can’t take any Prize cards for it.
Flavor-Text: This Ultra Beast is well enough liked to be chosen as a first partner in its own world.

Name: Poipole
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is Knocked Out, your opponent can’t take any Prize cards for it.
Flavor-Text: This Ultra Beast is well enough liked to be chosen as a first partner in its own world.

Name: Naganadel-GX
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Ultra Beasts in play.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Both players shuffle their Prize cards into their decks. Then, each player puts the top 3 cards of their deck face down as their Prize cards. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cubone
Effects: This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: The skull it wears on its head is that of its dead mother. According to some, it will evolve when it comes to terms with the pain of her death.

Name: Torterra
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
This attack does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Small Pokémon occasionally gather on its unmoving back to begin building their nests.

Name: Infernape
Ability-Effect: Put 6 damage counters instead of 2 on your opponent’s Burned Pokémon between turns.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: It tosses its enemies around with agility. It uses all its limbs to fight in its own unique style.

Name: Gible
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It skulks in caves, and when prey or an enemy passes by, it leaps out and chomps them. The force of its attack sometimes chips its teeth.

Name: Gabite
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Shiny objects are its passion. It can be found in its cave, scarcely moving, its gaze fixed on the jewels it’s amassed or Carbink it has caught.

Name: Garchomp
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If you played Cynthia from your hand during this turn, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The protuberances on its head serve as sensors. It can even detect distant prey.

Name: Croagunk
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

Name: Toxicroak
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
If any of your [P] Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack during their last turn, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its knuckle claws secrete a toxin so vile that even a scratch could prove fatal.

Name: Pancham
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [D] Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: There’s no point to the leaf in its mouth, aside from an effort to look cool. It’s mischievous, so it’s not well suited to inexperienced Trainers.

Name: Binacle
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: They stretch and then contract, yanking their rocks along with them in bold hops. They eat seaweed that washes up on the shoreline.

Name: Barbaracle
Effects: If you have exactly 7 cards in your hand, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Barbaracle’s legs and hands have minds of their own, and they will move independently. But they usually follow the head’s orders.

Name: Tyrunt
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its immense jaws have enough destructive force that it can chew up an automobile. It lived 100 million years ago.

Name: Tyrantrum
Ability-Effect: As long as you don’t have more Pokémon in play than your opponent, this Pokémon’s attacks do 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance), and it takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Nothing could stop this Pokémon 100 million years ago, so it behaved like a king.

Name: Hawlucha
Effects: This attack does 20 less damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: With its wings, it controls its position in the air. It likes to attack from above, a maneuver that is difficult to defend against.

Name: Zygarde
Ability-Effect: Damage from this Pokémon’s attacks isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Effects: If your opponent has an Ultra Beast in play, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This is Zygarde’s form when about 10% of its cells have been gathered. It runs across the land at speeds greater than 60 mph.

Name: Zygarde
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
If you have used your GX attack, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It’s thought to be monitoring the ecosystem. There are rumors that even greater power lies hidden within it.

Name: Zygarde-GX
Effects: Attach 2 [F] Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX during your opponent’s next turn. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Diancie Prism Star
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, your [F] Pokémon’s attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Heal 30 damage from each of your Benched Pokémon.
Prism-Star-Rule: You can’t have more than 1 [*] card with the same name in your deck. If a [*] card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.
Flavor-Text: It can instantly create many diamonds by compressing the carbon in the air between its hands.

Name: Rockruff
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has lived with people since times long ago. It can sense when its Trainer is in the dumps and will stick close by its Trainer’s side.

Name: Rockruff
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has lived with people since times long ago. It can sense when its Trainer is in the dumps and will stick close by its Trainer’s side.

Name: Lycanroc
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Bathed in the setting sun of evening, Lycanroc has undergone a special kind of evolution. An intense fighting spirit underlies its calmness.

Name: Buzzwole
Effects: If your opponent has exactly 4 Prize cards remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: A mysterious life-form called an Ultra Beast. Witnesses saw it pulverize a dump truck with a single punch.

Name: Buzzwole
Effects: If your opponent has exactly 4 Prize cards remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: A mysterious life-form called an Ultra Beast. Witnesses saw it pulverize a dump truck with a single punch.

Name: Pangoro
Effects: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 50 more damage, and both Active Pokémon are now Confused.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: From the slight twitches of its bamboo leaf, it deduces its opponent’s movements. It’s eager to tussle but kindhearted toward its companions.

Name: Yveltal-GX
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has exactly 4 damage counters on it, that Pokémon is Knocked Out. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Guzzlord
Effects: If you have exactly 2, 4, or 6 Prize cards remaining, discard the top 10 cards of your deck.
Flavor-Text: A dangerous Ultra Beast, it appears to be eating constantly, but for some reason its droppings have never been found.

Name: Guzzlord
Effects: If you have exactly 2, 4, or 6 Prize cards remaining, discard the top 10 cards of your deck.
Flavor-Text: A dangerous Ultra Beast, it appears to be eating constantly, but for some reason its droppings have never been found.

Name: Empoleon
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s).
Discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The three horns that extend from its beak attest to its power. The leader has the biggest horns.

Name: Dialga-GX
Effects: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Take another turn after this one. (Skip the between-turns step.) (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Flabébé
Ability-Effect: If you go second, this Pokémon can evolve during your first turn.
Flavor-Text: When it finds a flower it likes, it dwells on that flower its whole life long. It floats in the wind’s embrace with an untroubled heart.

Name: Flabébé
Effects: Shuffle 3 in any combination of Pokémon and basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Flavor-Text: When it finds a flower it likes, it dwells on that flower its whole life long. It floats in the wind’s embrace with an untroubled heart.

Name: Floette
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. If you do, switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When the flowers of a well-tended flower bed bloom, it appears and celebrates with an elegant dance.

Name: Florges
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, put an Item card from your discard pile on top of your deck.
Effects: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from [N] Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: In times long past, governors of castles would invite Florges to create flower gardens to embellish the castle domains.

Name: Sylveon
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. You may discard a Supporter card you find there and use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack.
Flavor-Text: When this Pokémon sights its prey, it swirls its ribbonlike feelers as a distraction. A moment later, it pounces.

Name: Dedenne
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
If you have any [L] Pokémon on your Bench, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It uses its tail to absorb electricity from power plants or from outlets in houses, and then it fires the electricity from its whiskers.

Name: Klefki
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It inserts its horn into chinks in metal, absorbing metal ions. For some reason, it collects keys.

Name: Xerneas-GX
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Move all damage counters from each of your Pokémon to your opponent’s Active Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Goomy
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent’s Pokémon’s attacks cost [C] more.
Flavor-Text: Its source of protection is its slimy, germ-laden mucous membrane. Anyone who touches it needs some thorough hand-washing.

Name: Goomy
Effects: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its source of protection is its slimy, germ-laden mucous membrane. Anyone who touches it needs some thorough hand-washing.

Name: Sliggoo
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s mucous can dissolve anything. Toothless, it sprays mucous on its prey. Once they’re nicely dissolved, it slurps them up.

Name: Goodra
Ability-Effect: Whenever you attach a [W] Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon, heal 20 damage from it.
Effects: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It gets picked on because it’s meek. But then, whoever teased it gets to feel the full force of its horns and a good swatting from its thick tail.

Name: Ultra Necrozma-GX
Effects: Discard all basic [P] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

You can use this attack only if the total of both players’ remaining Prize cards is 6 or less. Put 6 damage counters on each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Arceus Prism Star
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s attacks, except damage, done to this Pokémon.
Effects: You can use this attack only if you have [G], [W], and [L] Pokémon on your Bench. Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Prism-Star-Rule: You can’t have more than 1 [*] card with the same name in your deck. If a [*] card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.
Flavor-Text: It is told in mythology that this Pokémon was born before the universe even existed.

Name: Bunnelby
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It has ears like shovels. Digging holes strengthens its ears so much that they can sever thick roots effortlessly.

Name: Diggersby
Effects: You may do 40 more damage. If you do, discard the top 2 cards of your deck.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: As powerful as an excavator, its ears can reduce dense bedrock to rubble. When it’s finished digging, it lounges lazily.

Name: Furfrou
Effects: You may draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: Historically, in the Kalos region, these Pokémon were the designated guardians of the king.

Name: Noibat
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand. Discard all Item cards you find there.
Flavor-Text: Even a robust wrestler will become dizzy and unable to stand when exposed to its 200,000-hertz ultrasonic waves.

Name: Noivern
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Confused, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The ultrasonic waves it emits from its ears can reduce a large boulder to pebbles. It swoops out of the dark to attack.

Description: You can play this card only if your opponent has exactly 3 or 4 Prize cards remaining.Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Ultra Beasts. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.
